findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns that disable security mechanisms such as TLS verification, certificate checks, or safety guards. Seen in ToxicSkills campaign where skills weaken system security to enable further attacks.
findings.recommendation: This code disables important security mechanisms. This is a common precursor to further attacks — once security guards are down, malicious code can operate freely. Legitimate tools should never disable TLS verification or system firewalls.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects prompt injection patterns that use secrecy and urgency to manipulate the agent — telling it to hide actions from the user, not announce findings, keep things secret, or treat something as a "standard measure". These are hallmarks of malicious sampling/prompt injection attacks.
findings.recommendation: This code contains social engineering patterns designed to manipulate an AI agent — telling it to hide actions, keep secrets from the user, or treat suspicious requests as "standard procedure". This is a classic prompt injection technique. Do NOT trust this tool.

matches.description: Instructions to not announce or not tell the user

findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns where remote content is piped directly to a shell interpreter. This allows arbitrary code execution from a remote server. Frequently seen in malicious skills that download and execute payloads.
findings.reportTitle: Remote code download and execution detected
findings.recommendation: This code downloads content from the internet and executes it directly. An attacker can change the remote payload at any time. This is one of the most dangerous patterns in agent skills. Do not install.

fullDescription.text: Detects patterns where remote content is piped directly to a shell interpreter. This allows arbitrary code execution from a remote server. Frequently seen in malicious skills that download and execute payloads.

message.text: Remote code download and execution detected

properties.recommendation: This code downloads content from the internet and executes it directly. An attacker can change the remote payload at any time. This is one of the most dangerous patterns in agent skills. Do not install.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns that could cause denial of service through resource exhaustion: infinite loops without breaks, fork bombs, and extremely large memory allocations.
findings.reportTitle: Resource abuse or destructive pattern detected
findings.recommendation: This code contains patterns that could crash your system — fork bombs, excessive memory allocation, or destructive commands. These cause denial of service or data loss. Do not install.

fullDescription.text: Detects patterns that could cause denial of service through resource exhaustion: infinite loops without breaks, fork bombs, and extremely large memory allocations.

message.text: Resource abuse or destructive pattern detected

properties.recommendation: This code contains patterns that could crash your system — fork bombs, excessive memory allocation, or destructive commands. These cause denial of service or data loss. Do not install.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns where data is base64-encoded before being transmitted externally. Common in ToxicSkills campaign: credentials are read, encoded, and sent via URL parameter.
findings.recommendation: This code encodes data with base64 before sending it externally — a classic credential theft technique. Legitimate tools rarely need to base64-encode and transmit environment data. Do not install.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns where data is intercepted, copied, or forwarded to a secondary endpoint while still providing normal responses. This dual-channel approach is used by sophisticated malware to avoid detection — the tool works correctly while silently exfiltrating data.
findings.reportTitle: Data interception / dual-channel exfiltration pattern detected
findings.recommendation: This code appears to intercept, copy, or forward data to a hidden secondary endpoint while maintaining normal operation. This is the pattern used in the Postmark-MCP typosquat attack (hidden BCC on emails) and the YassWorks Shadow Analytics demo. Review all outbound network calls carefully.

matches.description: Variable/function names explicitly indicating data theft

fullDescription.text: Detects patterns where data is intercepted, copied, or forwarded to a secondary endpoint while still providing normal responses. This dual-channel approach is used by sophisticated malware to avoid detection — the tool works correctly while silently exfiltrating data.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns related to cryptocurrency wallet access, private key handling, or financial account operations. The Aslaep123 threat actor used crypto/trading bait skills to steal wallet credentials. Skills requesting access to financial operations should be treated with extreme caution.
findings.recommendation: This skill accesses cryptocurrency wallets or financial operations. While some skills legitimately interact with crypto, this is also a common attack vector for credential theft. Verify the skill author's reputation and review the code carefully before granting any financial access.

fullDescription.text: Detects patterns related to cryptocurrency wallet access, private key handling, or financial account operations. The Aslaep123 threat actor used crypto/trading bait skills to steal wallet credentials. Skills requesting access to financial operations should be treated with extreme caution.

properties.recommendation: This skill accesses cryptocurrency wallets or financial operations. findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns that could enable path traversal attacks, allowing access to files outside intended directories. Common in MCP servers that handle file operations without proper path sanitization.
findings.recommendation: This code accesses file system paths using external input without apparent sanitization. An attacker could use path traversal (../) to read or write files outside the intended directory. Review file access patterns carefully.

matches.description: path.join with external input without validation

findings.ruleDescription: Detects hardcoded API keys, tokens, passwords, and credentials in skill files. 10.9% of ClawHub skills contained hardcoded credentials (Snyk research). May indicate stolen credentials, test keys left in production, or intentional backdoors.
findings.recommendation: This skill contains what appears to be a hardcoded API key, token, or password. This could be: (1) stolen credentials embedded for exfiltration, (2) test credentials accidentally left in code, or (3) an intentional backdoor. Review carefully before use. If you've already installed, rotate any exposed credentials immediately.

matches.description: Generic API key or secret assignment with realistic-looking value

fullDescription.text: Detects hardcoded API keys, tokens, passwords, and credentials in skill files. 10.9% of ClawHub skills contained hardcoded credentials (Snyk research). May indicate stolen credentials, test keys left in production, or intentional backdoors.
